title: Climate Impacts on Agriculture
description: What are the projected climate change impacts on crop yields in the Midwest over the next 30 years?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed studies, simulation reports, or empirical analyses that quantify how projected temperature and precipitation changes will affect yield variability, yield per hectare, and crop resilience for key staples (e.g., corn and soybean). Documents should include statistical significance, model validation, and policy implications, rather than anecdotal or non‑scientific commentary.

title: Universal Basic Income Impact
description: What is the effect of universal basic income on employment participation rates among low‑income adults in European Union countries?
narrative: The user is a policy analyst seeking peer‑reviewed empirical studies that compare employment participation across EU countries after introduction of universal basic income pilots or programs. Relevant documents must provide longitudinal or repeat‑measurement data; they should focus explicitly on low‑income adult populations and include quantitative labor market outcome metrics. Exclude qualitative case studies, short news articles, or studies that only cover non‑EU jurisdictions.

title: Eco-Friendly Urban Farming
description: How can cities implement sustainable indoor farms to boost local food security?
narrative: The user seeks practical guidance on establishing indoor/vertical farms in dense urban settings. Relevance requires information on modular greenhouse designs, renewable energy integration, cost‑effectiveness, zoning & policy considerations, successful case studies, and impacts on local food supply chains. Articles that provide technical details, financial analyses, and real‑world examples are considered useful; purely historical or unrelated content is excluded.

title: Ethical AI for AVs
description: What ethical frameworks guide the development and deployment of AI systems in autonomous vehicles, and how are they implemented in practice today?
narrative: The user seeks recent policy documents, industry case studies, and academic analyses that describe how automotive companies operationalize ethical guidelines in safety-critical AI systems, focusing on practical implementation rather than theoretical discussion.

title: Arctic Marine Impact
description: What are the latest research findings on how climate change is altering marine biodiversity in the Arctic region?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date, peer‑reviewed studies that quantify changes in species composition, distribution, and ecological interactions in Arctic marine ecosystems due to warming temperatures, sea‑ice loss, and acidification. Relevant documents should include field surveys, modeling analyses, and conservation assessments. Papers that discuss only general climate change impacts, terrestrial ecosystems, or historical Arctic ice without biological focus should be considered irrelevant.

title: Urban Mobility Reform
description: What evidence-based policies can effectively reduce car traffic and emissions in medium-sized European cities?
narrative: The user is looking for studies, reports, or policy briefs that evaluate specific interventions—such as congestion pricing, improved public transit options, cycling infrastructure, or mixed-use zoning—demonstrating measurable decreases in vehicle counts, emissions, or travel times in cities with populations between 200,000 and 800,000. Relevance is judged by the study’s methodological rigor, geographic focus, and the practicality of policy implementation.

title: Predictive Policing Data Governance
description: What are the ethical implications and governance frameworks for using anonymized citizen data in predictive policing?
narrative: The user is a policy analyst seeking recent academic, governmental, and NGO reports that explore the privacy risks, oversight mechanisms, and case studies associated with predictive policing systems that rely on anonymized citizen data. Relevant documents should analyze ethical considerations, data governance models, and empirical outcomes of these systems. Documents that discuss general data privacy, unrelated policing methodologies, or purely technical implementation details without reference to governance or ethics are considered irrelevant.

title: AI Ethics in Healthcare
description: What are the latest guidelines and case studies on ethical use of AI in healthcare systems?
narrative: The user seeks recent academic and policy documents outlining regulatory frameworks, ethical principles, and real-world implementations of AI in clinical settings, and discloses that non-healthcare AI ethical discussions are irrelevant.

title: Concrete Slump Testing
description: What methods exist for accurately measuring slump in high-strength concrete mixes?
narrative: A civil engineer needs accurate slump measurement for high-strength concrete mixes (≈14000 psi). The user seeks detailed test protocols, suitable equipment, and adaptations for low-water/high-admixture mixes to ensure compliance with ASTM/ACI standards and consistent mix performance.

title: Desalination Public Partnerships
description: What are the most successful public‑private models for large‑scale seawater desalination projects worldwide?
narrative: I am researching how governments and private firms collaborate to build and operate seawater desalination facilities. Relevant sources should provide real‑world examples, explain the governance structures, financing arrangements, and performance outcomes. I need evidence of measurable success such as cost per cubic meter, water quality, operational sustainability, and lessons learned.

title: Urban Heat Island Mitigation
description: Which reflective roofing materials are most effective at reducing urban heat island intensity in midsized cities?
narrative: The user is a city planner investigating evidence‑based options for rooftop coatings. Relevant documents should provide quantitative temperature reductions, material durability, cost analysis, and installation guidelines from peer‑reviewed journals, industry reports, or municipal case studies. Materials beyond common white paint, such as engineered cool roofs and greenhouse‑roofing systems, should be considered.

title: Smart City Waste Management
description: What are the best practices and emerging technologies for managing waste in smart cities, including data integration, IoT sensors, and citizen engagement strategies?
narrative: The user is researching efficient, technology‑driven waste management solutions for urban environments aiming to reduce costs and environmental impact while enhancing citizen participation; relevant documents discuss integrated systems, case studies, regulatory frameworks, or comparative analyses of technologies.

title: NGO Crisis Strategies
description: What strategies have NGOs employed to manage sudden crises like supply chain disruptions or political instability?
narrative: I seek detailed analyses of NGO crisis response plans, focusing on effectiveness, adaptability, and reported outcomes in real‑life scenarios. Relevance requires evidence from case studies, reports, or scholarly evaluations that describe implemented measures, lessons learned, and measurable results.

title: AI‑Assisted Clinical Decision Support
description: I want to know which AI tools have proven effective in supporting diagnostic decisions for early‑stage lung cancer in outpatient settings.
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based information on machine learning models that are integrated into electronic health records to improve radiologist interpretation accuracy and reduce time to diagnosis. Relevant documents should report validated clinical trials, comparative studies, or implementation frameworks for such decision support systems, focusing on outpatient settings and early‑stage lung cancer. They should include performance metrics (sensitivity, specificity, time savings), workflow integration details, and patient outcome data.

title: Urban Sustainability Metrics
description: What are the most effective urban sustainability metrics used by European cities in the last decade?
narrative: The user is an urban planner looking for concrete, evaluable indicators to assess and compare sustainability performance across European municipalities. Relevance requires documents that define, explain, or apply metrics such as CO₂ emissions per capita, green space per capita, indoor environmental quality, waste recycling rates, or similar, based on credible municipal reports, academic studies, or policy analyses published between 2013 and 2023.

title: Rooftop Garden Best Practices
description: What are the best practices for implementing rooftop gardens in densely populated cities?
narrative: The user seeks actionable guidance and proven methodologies to design, build, and maintain rooftop gardens in urban environments. Relevance should be limited to peer‑reviewed studies, case reports, and technical manuals that address design criteria, cost‑effectiveness, and environmental impact.

title: Multilingual Hate Speech
description: Identify recent studies on detecting hate speech across multiple languages in social media, emphasizing performance metrics and large-scale datasets.
narrative: The user is an academic researcher who needs up-to-date papers (post‑2018) that focus specifically on hate‑speech detection on platforms such as Twitter or Reddit. Relevant documents must: include at least two distinct languages (e.g., English + Arabic or Spanish); describe a dataset of ≥1 million labeled posts; employ deep‑learning or advanced NLP techniques; and report clear evaluation metrics (accuracy, F1, recall). Brief reviews or surveys that merely summarize prior work without new empirical results are considered irrelevant.

title: M&A Impact on Brand Equity
description: How do major mergers between consumer packaged goods companies affect the perceived brand equity of the acquired brands?
narrative: A researcher or marketing strategist is looking for evidence that specifically examines changes in brand equity—such as consumer perception, brand valuation, and sales performance—before and after large mergers (e.g., Kraft Heinz). Relevance requires case studies, consumer survey data, brand equity metrics, and financial analyses directly tied to the merger event, while excluding general corporate biographies or unrelated business profiles.

title: Remote Work & Urban Space
description: What trends have emerged in how remote work has affected commercial office space demand in major U.S. cities over the past decade?
narrative: The user seeks analytical evidence of shifts in office vacancy rates, rental prices, and zoning changes in U.S. metropolitan areas from 2010 to 2024, with a focus on comparing pre‑ and post‑COVID‑19 data and highlighting any municipal policy initiatives related to remote work.

title: Sustainable Urban Farming
description: What best practices and technologies enable efficient urban farms that minimize environmental impact?
narrative: The user wants detailed, actionable information on how to create and run successful urban farms that are ecologically sound, economically viable, and socially inclusive. Relevant documents should include case studies, technological solutions (e.g., vertical hydroponics, aquaponics, renewable energy integration), and policy guidance for urban agriculture. Exclude general gardening tips, purely theoretical sustainability discussions, or non-urban farming methods.

title: AI Fairness Metrics
description: Which metrics can comprehensively evaluate fairness of an AI model across different demographic groups?
narrative: The user is developing an AI system and requires a detailed comparison of fairness metrics. Relevance criteria include technical explanations of metrics such as demographic parity, equalized odds, predictive parity, disparate impact, and calibration; mathematical formulations; discussions of strengths, weaknesses, and empirical use cases; and considerations of trade‑offs. Content unrelated to AI fairness or containing only generic information about services, hotels, or resorts is considered non‑relevant.

title: Sustainable Farming Techniques
description: What are the most effective regenerative agriculture practices for small-scale farms in temperate regions?
narrative: The user is a small farm operator looking for science-based methods to improve soil health, reduce inputs, and increase yield. Relevant documents should describe techniques that have peer-reviewed evidence, can be implemented within a 1–2 year timeframe, and do not require large capital investments.

title: Remote Workforce Management
description: What actionable strategies can organizations adopt to effectively manage employees working remotely while preserving productivity and team cohesion?
narrative: The user needs evidence‑based, practical guidance on supervising distributed teams. Relevance criteria include detailed case studies, empirical research, managerial frameworks, and best‑practice checklists that address communication, performance monitoring, culture building, and work‑life balance. Documents that merely mention telecommuting in passing or provide unsubstantiated claims should be considered less relevant.

title: Urban Rooftop Greenhouses
description: What are the most effective design and operational strategies for rooftop greenhouse systems in densely populated cities?
narrative: A city planner is exploring the feasibility of rooftop agriculture within a major metropolitan area. The user needs technical design guidelines, cost‑benefit analyses, energy‑efficiency data, zoning and permitting information, and documented pilot projects. Relevant documents should provide peer‑reviewed studies, engineering manuals, case reports, and policy papers specifically addressing rooftop greenhouse installations in urban contexts.

title: Po River Grounding
description: What grounding techniques are suitable for electrical systems in properties along the Po River to protect against floodwater infiltration?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on equipment grounding, bonding jumper selection, and electrode conductor installation for buildings in the Po River floodplain, including compliance with electrical codes and local flood‑control regulations.

title: AI Ethics in Automation
description: What are the main ethical concerns and regulatory guidelines addressing decision‑making algorithms in autonomous vehicles?
narrative: The user is researching how AI ethics principles and regulatory frameworks intersect to guide the design and deployment of decision‑making algorithms in autonomous vehicles. Documents are relevant if they discuss moral dilemmas, liability issues, transparency requirements, and policy guidelines from industry groups and governments.

title: Ligament vs Tendon
description: How do ligaments and tendons differ in structure, function, and clinical significance within the musculoskeletal system?
narrative: The user is a graduate anatomy researcher seeking a precise, clinically-oriented comparison of ligaments and tendons, including their collagen composition, mechanical properties, attachment sites, role in joint stability versus muscle force transmission, and common injury patterns. Relevance requires documents that detail these distinctions with supporting biomechanical or pathological evidence, and excludes generic overviews, unrelated musculoskeletal components, or non-expert summaries.

title: Personalized Oncology Protocol
description: What are the current best practices for integrating whole-genome sequencing into personalized cancer treatment while ensuring regulatory compliance and patient data privacy?
narrative: The user seeks contemporary guidance or case studies that link genomic testing, clinical decision-making, regulatory approvals, and privacy frameworks in oncology. Relevant documents should describe recent (last five years) best practices, guidelines, or policy documents; documents that are purely technical, unrelated to oncology, or do not address both regulatory compliance and data privacy can be ignored.

title: Property Tax Burden
description: What is the relationship between state property taxes and median household income across the United States?
narrative: The user seeks a comparative analysis of property tax rates as a percentage of median income for each state, aiming to identify states with the highest and lowest burdens.

title: Rural Migration Climate
description: What are the patterns of rural population migration in response to climate change impacts across developing nations?
narrative: The user seeks current, evidence‑based information on how climate‑related factors (e.g., drought, sea‑level rise, extreme weather) are driving rural communities to relocate in low‑ and middle‑income countries. Relevant documents should contain empirical data, case studies, or policy analyses that link climate variables to migration flows or livelihood changes in rural settings.

title: WWII Draft Cards
description: I need to find World War II draft registration cards for people born in New York between 1920 and 1940.
narrative: The user is a genealogist looking for primary source documents that confirm an individual's draft registration status during World War II.  Relevant records must be official draft cards, show the registrant’s birth year (1920‑1940), place of birth/state (New York), and date of registration.  Exclude summaries, secondary references, or documents covering other periods or states.

title: Blockchain for Supply Chain
description: What are proven blockchain technologies that improve traceability, reduce costs, and ensure compliance in global supply chains?
narrative: The user is a supply‑chain manager evaluating decentralized ledger solutions; they need evidence‑based case studies, implementation details, ROI metrics, and compliance notes for large‑scale, multi‑party operations. Relevance means documents that describe real deployments, provide measurable benefits, and cover regulatory aspects.

title: Single-Use Plastic Impact
description: What are the environmental and socioeconomic impacts of single-use plastic consumption in urban regions?
narrative: User seeks a comprehensive analysis covering lifecycle environmental burdens, local policy implications, and economic effects, with emphasis on post‑2020 data and case studies in major metropolitan areas.

title: Urban Smart Housing
description: What are the main economic, environmental, and social factors that influence the adoption of smart home technologies in high‑rise urban apartments?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive analysis of drivers and barriers to smart home deployment in urban settings, focusing on cost, energy savings, privacy concerns, and regulatory incentives. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, case studies, and policy reviews that illustrate how these factors interact in real‑world deployment contexts.

title: Smarter Urban Mobility
description: What data sources and visualization techniques best support real-time urban mobility planning for cities battling traffic congestion?
narrative: The user is a city planner seeking detailed guidance on integrating live traffic, public transit, and pedestrian flow data into interactive dashboards that inform policy decisions. Relevant documents should cover recent case studies, open data initiatives, advanced GIS tools, privacy best practices, and metrics for evaluating success.

title: CO2 Reduction Case Studies
description: Find detailed case studies of manufacturing companies that achieved at least a 10% reduction in CO₂ emissions over a five-year period.
narrative: The user is looking for concrete, evidence-based examples of manufacturing firms that have quantified a minimum 10% CO₂ emissions cut across a five-year span. Relevance requires that the source includes the company name, industry sector, exact timeframe, measured results, the strategies and technologies implemented, and some form of external validation or audit. Documents that present only theoretical models, general sustainability overviews, or companies without clear, documented emission figures should be considered irrelevant.

title: Hybrid Cloud Container Security
description: What are the recommended best practices and emerging tools for securing container orchestration across hybrid cloud environments?
narrative: The user is an IT security architect evaluating solutions and wants documents that detail implementation guides, case studies, and compliance mappings for Kubernetes, Docker Swarm, and OpenShift in hybrid settings. Relevance requires covering hybrid‑cloud specifics, best‑practice guidelines, and references to industry standards such as NIST, CIS benchmarks, and vendor solutions.

title: AI in Personalized Medicine
description: How can artificial intelligence tools be effectively integrated into personalized medicine to improve diagnosis and treatment plans?
narrative: The user is a medical researcher seeking to understand current applications, best practices, and emerging trends where AI is applied to tailor healthcare decisions to individual patient data. Relevance criteria include scholarly articles, case studies, and technical guides that discuss AI algorithms, data integration, ethical considerations, and measurable clinical outcomes, while excluding general overviews or non-clinical AI references.

title: Minority STEM Mentoring
description: How can universities improve mentorship programs for minority students in STEM fields?
narrative: The user seeks evidence-based strategies and best practices for university-level mentorship initiatives targeting minority students pursuing STEM degrees. Relevant documents should include studies, university policy documents, program evaluations, and case studies that discuss program design, implementation, measurable outcomes, challenges, and recommendations for improving diversity and inclusion in STEM education.

title: Wedding Dress Budget
description: What is a reasonable budget range for a wedding dress considering total wedding costs and multi-use plans?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on how much to spend on a wedding dress, factoring in overall wedding expenses, possible multi-use scenarios, and how the price relates to alterations, sizing, and durability. Relevant evidence should include estimates of dress costs as a percentage of total wedding cost, examples of budgets for different guest counts, and tips on selecting gowns that can be reused or repurposed.

title: Quantum Entanglement Studies
description: What are the latest peer‑reviewed studies on quantum entanglement in quantum computing?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date research articles, reviews, and conference papers (2020–2025) that discuss quantum entanglement phenomena, especially their practical application to quantum computing and theoretical advances. Non‑scholarly publications and older sources are considered irrelevant.

title: Renewable Energy Adoption
description: Which recent policies encourage renewable energy use for small businesses?
narrative: The user seeks practical guidance for a small‑business owner to evaluate and implement renewable energy solutions. Relevant documents should provide up‑to‑date policy descriptions, eligibility criteria for grants or tax incentives, case studies of successfully adopted projects, cost‑benefit analyses, and implementation timelines. Irrelevant content such as general health or unrelated medication information should be excluded.

title: AI Healthcare Support
description: What AI tools are currently being used in healthcare to improve diagnostic accuracy and patient outcomes, and what evidence exists to support their efficacy?
narrative: The user is a medical researcher seeking detailed, evidence‑based information on existing AI applications in clinical settings. They require data on tools that have been validated through reputable studies or regulatory approvals, including performance metrics, real‑world case studies, and identified implementation barriers.

title: Historical Climate Data
description: Where can I find detailed, date‑specific climate data for the 20th and 21st centuries from reliable government sources?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, time‑stamped climate records—including temperature, precipitation, and extreme events—curated by official meteorological agencies. Relevant documents must provide geographic coverage, resolution, and proper documentation, and should exclude non‑verified or non‑scientific sources.

title: Ethical AI Healthcare
description: What are best practices for healthcare organizations to ethically integrate AI into patient care while ensuring regulatory compliance and preserving patient trust?
narrative: A healthcare administrator seeks guidance on responsibly adopting AI tools, including technical, ethical, regulatory, and privacy considerations. Relevance will be judged on sources that provide concrete case studies, policy frameworks, impact assessments, and actionable recommendations for implementation.

title: Urban Microclimate Modeling
description: Which remote sensing techniques are most effective for measuring urban microclimate variations over a 24‑hour period?
narrative: The user is a graduate student researching urban heat island mitigation, seeking studies that use satellite or aerial data to observe temperature, humidity, and wind patterns in urban environments on a sub‑diurnal timescale. Relevance requires documents that report on remote sensing methodology, multitemporal analysis, and validation against ground stations, while excluding generic climate models or non‑urban studies.

title: Print Sustainability
description: What sustainable practices can small book publishers adopt to reduce environmental impact while maintaining print quality?
narrative: The user is a small‑scale book publisher looking for evidence‑based, cost‑effective, and manageable environmental strategies. Relevance criteria include feasibility for small operations, impact on waste and emissions, preservation of paper and ink quality, and up‑to‑date research or case studies in eco‑friendly printing.

title: Digital Library Licensing
description: What are the primary legal and practical considerations libraries face when negotiating digital licensing agreements with publishers?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on best practices, statutory compliance, cost models, and negotiation strategies for digital resources in library settings; documents offering general marketing or unrelated technological details are considered irrelevant.

title: Code‑Switching Dynamics
description: What factors influence the frequency and contextual usage of code‑switching among bilingual college students during academic conversations?
narrative: The user seeks academic literature that examines sociolinguistic variables (e.g., subject area, interlocutor gender, perceived power dynamics) affecting code‑switching patterns in spoken undergraduate discussions, with a preference for recent empirical studies (2015‑2023) and peer‑reviewed journal articles, excluding general language learning surveys.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: What are the environmental, economic, and social impacts of installing green roofs in major US cities?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, city reports, or industry white papers that analyze how green roofs influence heat island mitigation, stormwater management, energy consumption, and community well‑being. Relevant documents should include quantitative studies, policy evaluations, and case examples from cities such as New York, Los Angeles, or Chicago, addressing both benefits and challenges of implementation. Documents that merely name green roofs without detailed analysis or that focus on unrelated horticulture topics are not relevant.

title: Sustainable Urban Mobility
description: What evidence‑based interventions can cities implement to promote sustainable urban mobility while reducing carbon emissions and traffic congestion?
narrative: The user seeks academic and policy research on practical, scalable strategies—such as bike‑sharing programs, pedestrian zoning, public transit electrification, and integrated mobility platforms—that demonstrate measurable outcomes in emissions reduction, accessibility, and cost‑effectiveness. Relevant documents should include case studies, evaluation reports, and comparative analyses of interventions across diverse urban contexts, and should exclude purely theoretical proposals or anecdotal accounts without evidence of impact.

title: AI Mental Health
description: What are the current applications and outcomes of AI-driven tools in treating mental health disorders?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, empirical studies, and policy analyses that examine the effectiveness, ethical implications, and clinical outcomes of AI-based interventions in psychiatry—such as chatbots, diagnostic algorithms, and personalized therapy recommendations—highlighting both successes and limitations.

title: Urban Agriculture Funding
description: Which federal and municipal grants or subsidies are currently available to support urban farming projects?
narrative: The user wants up-to‑date, U.S.-centric information on eligibility criteria, application deadlines, and the types of projects eligible for public funding, explicitly excluding private foundations or international programs.

title: Digital Divide in Higher Ed
description: How does the digital divide affect university students' academic outcomes during the COVID‑19 pandemic?
narrative: The user wants evidence on disparities in technology access, online learning resources, and institutional support for students, seeking data linking digital connectivity to grades, course completion, and retention rates during the pandemic.
